attribution: Underlying drug-condition pairs are clinical facts. Original brief notes by the project author. See AGS publication for the authoritative list and rationale: https://americangeriatrics.org/

pairs.note: Strongly anticholinergic. Risk of confusion, urinary retention, falls. Avoid for sleep and for cold/cough in older adults when possible.
Long-acting benzodiazepine. Increased risk of cognitive impairment, falls, fractures. Avoid as first line.
Risk of acute kidney injury and fluid retention. Avoid chronic use.
Long-acting sulfonylurea. Higher risk of prolonged hypoglycemia. Prefer shorter-acting alternatives.
